action.reasoning: Observation: iter4 ran partial training (loss 4.9->1.6 over ~8 epochs) with new attention model but exited with code 1 due to truncated model.py (MathExprRecognizer missing) + train.py (incomplete best-saving logic), plus debug failed on old checkpoint mismatch (LSTM shapes 256 vs 768). Val exprate still 0 from prior. Analysis: Attention+spatial features from ResNet are appropriate for 2D math (loss decreasing confirms learning); truncation and checkpoint conflict are root causes from incremental edits. Debug preds will now be more meaningful. Decision: Supply complete fixed model.py (full recognizer wrapper + consistent inits) and train.py (complete training loop, best.pt save on val exprate, 12 epochs given ~25s/epoch timing), minor debug/predict tweaks for robustness; run full train+debug then submit_eval on best.pt for first positive baseline score + updated timing.
